evals.prompt: I keep asking Claude to review my pull requests and it floods my main chat with diffs. Can you set me up a dedicated agent that just reviews recent code changes for quality and security and reports back, read-only?
evals.expected_output: A ready-to-save .claude/agents/ file for a focused, read-only code-reviewer subagent: kebab-case name, a specific 'when to use' description, tools limited to Read/Grep/Glob (+ Bash for git diff, no Write/Edit), a deliberately chosen model with justification, and a system-prompt body following the role -> when-invoked steps -> checklist -> output anatomy.
expectations: Output is a complete agent file with valid YAML frontmatter (name + description present)
name is kebab-case and description states when Claude should delegate
tools are least-privilege: includes Read/Grep/Glob, excludes Write and Edit
model field is set and the choice is explained to the user
Body follows the role -> 'When invoked' steps -> checklist -> output-format anatomy
Tells the user where to save the file and that a session restart loads disk edits

evals.prompt: Make me a fast, cheap agent that just explores big codebases and finds where things are defined. It should never modify files. I don't care about deep reasoning, I want it quick.
evals.expected_output: A read-only explorer subagent recommending model: haiku for speed/cost, tools restricted to Read/Grep/Glob (no Write/Edit/Bash unless justified), with the model recommendation explicitly justified by the 'fast and cheap' requirement.
expectations: Recommends model: haiku and justifies it from the fast/cheap requirement
description makes the agent's exploration purpose and when-to-use clear

evals.prompt: I want a debugging agent that can actually fix failing tests in our Spring Boot service, not just point at them. Use whatever model my session is on.
evals.expected_output: A debugger subagent that includes Edit/Write/Bash (because it fixes code), model: inherit (honoring 'whatever model my session is on'), and a body with a diagnosis-to-verification workflow.
expectations: model is set to inherit, honoring the user's 'use my session model' request
tools include Edit/Write and Bash because the agent must modify and run code
Body includes a root-cause-first debugging workflow ending in verification
